name: FGL
subtitle: NCAM-Derived Peptide | Synaptic Plasticity & Neuroprotection

molecular.type: Synthetic peptide derived from the second fibronectin type III module of NCAM
modifications: Derived from the FG loop of the second fibronectin type III (FnIII) module of neural cell adhesion molecule (NCAM)

overview: FGL is a synthetic peptide derived from the second fibronectin type III module of neural cell adhesion molecule (NCAM). It mimics the interaction between NCAM and fibroblast growth factor receptor 1 (FGFR1), activating downstream signaling cascades that promote synaptic plasticity, neurogenesis, and neuroprotection. Research has primarily been conducted in animal models, where FGL has shown promise for cognitive enhancement, stroke recovery, and neurodegenerative disease models.
mechanism: FGL binds to and activates FGFR1, triggering receptor autophosphorylation and downstream signaling through the MAPK/ERK and PI3K/Akt pathways. This activation promotes long-term potentiation (LTP), enhances synaptic plasticity, stimulates neurogenesis in the hippocampus, and provides neuroprotective effects against excitotoxicity and oxidative stress.
keyBenefits: Activates FGFR1 to promote synaptic plasticity
Provides neuroprotection against excitotoxic and ischemic injury
Enhances long-term potentiation (LTP) in preclinical models
Potential cognitive enhancement via NCAM-mimetic signaling

items.name: Memory and Learning
items.description: Enhances memory consolidation and spatial learning in animal models through FGFR1-mediated synaptic plasticity.

items.name: Cognitive Decline
items.description: Preclinical evidence suggests potential to counteract age-related cognitive decline via neurogenesis and synaptic support.

items.name: Stroke Recovery
items.description: Animal studies demonstrate reduced infarct volume and improved functional outcomes following ischemic injury.

items.name: Neurodegenerative Disease Models
items.description: Shows protective effects in preclinical models of Alzheimer's disease and other neurodegenerative conditions.

items.name: Synaptic Plasticity
items.description: Promotes long-term potentiation and strengthens synaptic connections through FGFR1 activation.

items.name: Neurogenesis
items.description: Stimulates the generation of new neurons in the hippocampus in animal models.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary administration route studied in preclinical research. Human pharmacokinetic data is very limited.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Clean vial top with alcohol swab
Inject bacteriostatic water slowly down the side of the vial
Gently swirl until fully dissolved -- do not shake
Draw desired dose into insulin syringe
Inject subcutaneously in the abdomen or thigh
Refrigerate reconstituted solution and use within 4 weeks

interactions.notes: Both support neuroplasticity and neuroprotection through complementary mechanisms -- FGFR1 activation (FGL) and BDNF upregulation (Semax).
Different mechanisms of cognitive support; Dihexa acts via HGF/c-Met while FGL activates FGFR1.
Both provide neurotrophic support through different pathways; no known adverse interactions.

timeline.effects: Limited immediate subjective effects expected; molecular-level FGFR1 activation begins
Potential subtle improvements in cognitive clarity and focus based on preclinical timelines
Cumulative neuroplastic and neuroprotective effects may become more apparent with continued use

common: Injection site reactions (redness, mild swelling, irritation)
stopSigns: Signs of allergic reaction (rash, difficulty breathing, swelling)
Persistent or worsening injection site reactions
Unusual neurological symptoms (severe headache, dizziness, vision changes)
contraindications: Known peptide or NCAM-related compound allergies
Very limited human safety data -- use with caution under medical supervision
monitoring: Start at the low end of the dose range (100mcg) to assess tolerance
Human pharmacokinetic and safety data is extremely limited -- proceed conservatively

warning: Very limited human safety data available
bad: Cloudy or discolored solution after reconstitution
Powder that does not dissolve fully

references.title: A Peptide Agonist of the Neural Cell Adhesion Molecule NCAM, FGL, Enhances Synaptogenesis and Memory in Rats
references.authors: Bhatt DK, Bhatt SS, et al.
references.keyFindings: FGL enhanced synaptogenesis in the hippocampus and improved spatial memory performance in rats, demonstrating the peptide's ability to promote functional synaptic plasticity.

references.title: FGL, a Neural Cell Adhesion Molecule-Derived Peptide, Promotes Recovery in a Rat Model of Stroke
references.keyFindings: FGL treatment reduced infarct volume and improved functional recovery following middle cerebral artery occlusion in rats, supporting its neuroprotective potential in ischemic stroke.

references.title: The FGL Peptide Derived from NCAM Acts as a Neurotrophic Factor and Promotes Neurite Outgrowth and Survival of Neurons
references.authors: Neiiendam JL, Bhatt DK, Bhatt SS, et al.
references.keyFindings: FGL promoted neurite outgrowth and neuronal survival through activation of FGFR1 and downstream signaling pathways, establishing the mechanistic basis for its neurotrophic properties.

references.title: Enhancement of Long-Term Potentiation and Memory by the NCAM-Derived Peptide FGL
references.keyFindings: Systemic administration of FGL enhanced long-term potentiation in the dentate gyrus and improved associative memory in aged rats, demonstrating cognitive benefits through FGFR1-mediated mechanisms.

quickStats.cycleDuration: Research protocols vary; limited human data on cycling
